pairedcountbrms 0.6.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
    • The anatomy of a paired count
  • Changelog

Convert native binegbin/bipois dpars to (M, f, delta) coordinates

Source: R/mfd.R
binegbin_dpars_to_mfd.Rd

Exact inverse of [binegbin_mfd_to_dpars()]. Reads the rate dpars `mu`, `lambdaem`, `lambdalb` back into the interpretable overall level `M`, congruence `f`, and method bias `delta`, optionally converting `shapes`/`shapex` back to SD-scale `kappas`/`kappax`.

Usage

binegbin_dpars_to_mfd(mu, lambdaem, lambdalb, shapes = NULL, shapex = NULL)

Arguments

mu

Shared-component rate.

lambdaem, lambdalb

The two excess rates.

shapes, shapex

Optional NB2 dispersions. If supplied, the returned list gains `kappas`/`kappax` (`= 1/sqrt(shape)`, so `shape = Inf` gives `0`).

Value

A named list of `M`, `f`, `delta`, plus `beta` (the bounded bias `tanh(delta)`), and `kappas`/`kappax` when `shapes`/`shapex` are supplied.

Details

Arguments are recycled to a common length, so this vectorises over posterior draws – e.g. to convert a whole posterior into interpretable coordinates.

**Boundary behaviour**, which the forward direction does not have:

* `lambdaem == lambdalb == 0` (perfect congruence, `f = 1`): the bias is genuinely unidentified – there is no excess to be biased – and `delta` is returned as `NA`, not `0`. Zero would assert an unbiased method, which the data at that point cannot support. * `M == 0` (nothing anywhere): `f` is undefined and returned as `NA`. * Exactly one excess rate `0`: `delta` is `+/-Inf`, the well-defined limit where one source never records an unshared event.

Because of the first case, round-tripping is exact everywhere except at `f = 1`, where `delta` cannot be recovered. Hold one coordinate system as the source of truth rather than repeatedly converting back and forth.

See also

[binegbin_mfd_to_dpars()], [binegbin()], [bipois()]

Examples

binegbin_dpars_to_mfd(mu = 8.04, lambdaem = 4.75, lambdalb = 3.17)
#> $M
#> [1] 12
#> 
#> $f
#> [1] 0.67
#> 
#> $delta
#> [1] 0.2022065
#> 
#> $beta
#> [1] 0.1994949
#> 

# Round trip
d <- binegbin_mfd_to_dpars(M = 12, f = 0.67, delta = 0.2)
binegbin_dpars_to_mfd(d$mu, d$lambdaem, d$lambdalb)[c("M", "f", "delta")]
#> $M
#> [1] 12
#> 
#> $f
#> [1] 0.67
#> 
#> $delta
#> [1] 0.2
#> 

# Perfect congruence: bias is unidentified, reported as NA
binegbin_dpars_to_mfd(mu = 12, lambdaem = 0, lambdalb = 0)$delta
#> [1] NA
